channel.title: Installing Simplex
channel.description: I gave a try at installing Simplex by `sudo apt install simplex ` but for some reason it doesn't start. ``` [user ~]% apt list --installed simplex simplex/now 6.4.11-1 amd64 [installed,local] ``` Is there some security feature in place that prevent it to execute (or a wrong ver.)?

item.title: Installing Simplex
item.dc:creator: SpecialBook
item.description:
flatpak version is successfully installed and working

Read full topic

item.title: Installing Simplex
item.dc:creator: exfil
item.description:
Honestly I tried Simplex the GUI version and I’m not sure what to think tbh I dont want spread FUD but certain things have me second guessing it.

Read full topic

item.title: Installing Simplex
item.dc:creator: arraybolt3
item.description:
You could use one of the releases from Github: Release v6.4.11 · simplex-chat/simplex-chat · GitHub From what I can tell, they use Github Actions to build the binaries, but have reproducible builds working for some of the binaries they produce, so they redo the build on a separate machine, verify it is identical, and then sign the binary, so you only end up trusting one party. The simplex-desktop-x86_64.AppImage file is hashed and signed, so it should be safe if you trust the Simplex developers and you verify the AppImage.

At least, I think that’s the case. There’s been some previous weirdness with signing and reproducible builds. I just asked on their Github if what I said above is right:

github.com/simplex-chat/simplex-chat

Signed GPG releases broken or dont make sense?

opened 07:44PM - 22 Jun 25 UTC
Qveen-dev

- **Steps** `gpg --import FB44AF81A45BDE327319797C85107E357D4A17FC.asc` ```con…sole bobross@localhost:~/Downloads$ gpg --verify _sha256sums.asc _sha256sums gpg: Signature made Tue 10 Jun 2025 09:17:37 PM UTC gpg: using RSA key FB44AF81A45BDE327319797C85107E357D4A17FC gpg: Good signature from "SimpleX Chat <chat@simplex.chat>" [unknown] gpg: WARNING: This key is not certified with a trusted signature! gpg: There is no indication that the signature belongs to the owner. Primary key fingerprint: FB44 AF81 A45B DE32 7319 797C 8510 7E35 7D4A 17FC ``` Ok, so the shasums is signed with good signature. \ Now in my case I go to get the shasum of the `simplex-desktop-x86_64.AppImage` ```console bobross@localhost:~/Downloads$ sha256sum simplex-desktop-x86_64.AppImage 7b288412619abb333c7d8c6c6cf8f97a071674ad08020cf37bb5cdb92fb7f26c simplex-desktop-x86_64.AppImage ``` Then check if it matched the signed file: ```console bobross@localhost:~/Downloads$ cat _sha256sums 84d88f8e9da985ef3eb38caf1bc958b6b6062f90bf1c8c3fd053cb8c92fba964 v6.3.5/simplex-chat-ubuntu-22_04-x86-64 695cfde8ecc3800f92a15876aeb45fb762032c1278dfb739275a3e7985b3277e v6.3.5/simplex-chat-ubuntu-24_04-x86-64 ``` - **No Trust** There is nothing go off for the hash that indicates trust and it looks like it's only the release thats signed. \ Typically you see a clear-signed file so I'm not sure how the project is signing the releases. *It's like signing a file that has no hash in it so whats the point???* ---- SHA256SUMS should have the hashes like this: ``` - SHA256SUMS 870f9397fee73132bda4015f0593f89f57d6e8a939ab6d88beb10a5a082d23b7 simplex-aarch64.apk 4e6ebb0ffe795d42adb261a694d9130333e7a7ea5ce8a56399ef4c23c5db31da simplex-armv7a.apk ab89fcdb8a45d45f9d92a591c582ccd439faf2f121db210b8d51cc562e1d0981 simplex-chat-macos-aarch64 cb2b981b8a8571351cc60236a62123a21da3ec5c5e3dcb367025ae6fa961c6c1 simplex-chat-macos-x86-64 84d88f8e9da985ef3eb38caf1bc958b6b6062f90bf1c8c3fd053cb8c92fba964 simplex-chat-ubuntu-22_04-x86-64 695cfde8ecc3800f92a15876aeb45fb762032c1278dfb739275a3e7985b3277e simplex-chat-ubuntu-24_04-x86-64 0b99ff2466ccd8128c20be838de6e7cfa2e182c86f4c7f47a4a67d5b15bd4a2b simplex-chat-windows-x86-64 a6f2dbe0b62270a65563f2899148841e768a14f8c36ce572aba0ea2d73493fb8 simplex-desktop-macos-aarch64.dmg edfe63e48c2f020e889f715a9fc76734ca5c70ae279677cae45c2f286ef8af1d simplex-desktop-macos-x86_64.dmg 010d32eb90f74ce8a035992eb2cbb40fb4405fdb74e06f09d4fb03985106d3a5 simplex-desktop-ubuntu-22_04-x86_64.deb 86dcae50239d386261a0276b97c35337c28769c55000c94e275441d03c247b0e simplex-desktop-ubuntu-24_04-x86_64.deb ae8adefaa45ace8b7ded7c7ddd56647246123fe764bee27e9a1f182d39448f25 simplex-desktop-windows-x86_64.msi 7b288412619abb333c7d8c6c6cf8f97a071674ad08020cf37bb5cdb92fb7f26c simplex-desktop-x86_64.AppImage 870f9397fee73132bda4015f0593f89f57d6e8a939ab6d88beb10a5a082d23b7 simplex.apk ``` No need for having _sha256sums.asc when you could just clear sign `gpg --clearsign` the hashes also - Example of SHA256SUMS ```gpg -----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE----- Hash: SHA512 - SHA256SUMS 870f9397fee73132bda4015f0593f89f57d6e8a939ab6d88beb10a5a082d23b7 simplex-aarch64.apk 4e6ebb0ffe795d42adb261a694d9130333e7a7ea5ce8a56399ef4c23c5db31da simplex-armv7a.apk ab89fcdb8a45d45f9d92a591c582ccd439faf2f121db210b8d51cc562e1d0981 simplex-chat-macos-aarch64 cb2b981b8a8571351cc60236a62123a21da3ec5c5e3dcb367025ae6fa961c6c1 simplex-chat-macos-x86-64 84d88f8e9da985ef3eb38caf1bc958b6b6062f90bf1c8c3fd053cb8c92fba964 simplex-chat-ubuntu-22_04-x86-64 695cfde8ecc3800f92a15876aeb45fb762032c1278dfb739275a3e7985b3277e simplex-chat-ubuntu-24_04-x86-64 0b99ff2466ccd8128c20be838de6e7cfa2e182c86f4c7f47a4a67d5b15bd4a2b simplex-chat-windows-x86-64 a6f2dbe0b62270a65563f2899148841e768a14f8c36ce572aba0ea2d73493fb8 simplex-desktop-macos-aarch64.dmg edfe63e48c2f020e889f715a9fc76734ca5c70ae279677cae45c2f286ef8af1d simplex-desktop-macos-x86_64.dmg 010d32eb90f74ce8a035992eb2cbb40fb4405fdb74e06f09d4fb03985106d3a5 simplex-desktop-ubuntu-22_04-x86_64.deb 86dcae50239d386261a0276b97c35337c28769c55000c94e275441d03c247b0e simplex-desktop-ubuntu-24_04-x86_64.deb ae8adefaa45ace8b7ded7c7ddd56647246123fe764bee27e9a1f182d39448f25 simplex-desktop-windows-x86_64.msi 7b288412619abb333c7d8c6c6cf8f97a071674ad08020cf37bb5cdb92fb7f26c simplex-desktop-x86_64.AppImage 870f9397fee73132bda4015f0593f89f57d6e8a939ab6d88beb10a5a082d23b7 simplex.apk -----BEGIN PGP SIGNATURE----- JYXjbz+DTQ4aUx/yhW8nOvBo+7YjVMTde8ZpdiFbtEb8sVtMDvvM7TK4kBw/O6lv rarbZJbzVCRP7q9OzQJlvD+Yi1ek+sQDa1aLSw2leLilJB4mm+C9KhAbqlBNz7+N9 TkjvM5r8dyzTQcMQeqB0t8oSsAftzHZk= ==H/vfJ5 -----END PGP SIGNATURE----- ```

Read full topic

item.title: Installing Simplex
item.dc:creator: devmaurice
item.description:
Is there a way to install the GUI version of SimpleX Chat that doesn’t rely on Flathub’s trust model (i.e., a third-party proxy that isn’t developer-signed) or manual installation?

Read full topic

item.title: Installing Simplex
item.dc:creator: remsa
item.description:
Ok, It seems it is actually the CLI version for terminal with no GUI

[user ~]% simplex-chat                
SimpleX Chat v6.4.10.0
db: /home/user/.simplex/simplex_v1_chat.db, /home/user/.simplex/simplex_v1_agent.db
direct network connection - use `/network` command or `-x` CLI option to connect via SOCKS5 at :9050
private message routing mode: unknown, fallback: protected
type "/help" or "/h" for usage info
No user profiles found, it will be created now.
Please choose your display name.
It will be sent to your contacts when you connect.
It is only stored on your device and you can change it later.

I thought it would have been the GUI version. Non entirely clear where to get the right one.

I had tried yesterday to install it via Flatpak flatpak install flathub chat.simplex.simplex but for some reason I got an error, something like a missing URL path.
UPDATE
It works today.
I have two now. I uninstalled the CLI.
Anyways, sudo apt install simplex is the CLI version.

Read full topic

item.title: Installing Simplex
item.dc:creator: arraybolt3
item.description:
What happens if you try to run Simplex in a terminal? Can you share the terminal output here? That will help us determine if the problem is on our end or is an upstream bug.

Read full topic

item.title: Installing Simplex
item.dc:creator: remsa
item.description:
I gave a try at installing Simplex by sudo apt install simplex but for some reason it doesn’t start.

[user ~]% apt list --installed simplex
simplex/now 6.4.11-1 amd64 [installed,local]

Is there some security feature in place that prevent it to execute (or a wrong ver.)?

Read full topic
